acid-splash.description: You hurl a bubble of acid. Choose one creature you can see within range, or choose two creatures you can see within range that are within 5 feet of each other. A target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d6 acid damage.
acid-splash.higherLevels: The spell's damage increases by 1d6 when you reach 5th level (2d6), 11th level (3d6), and 17th level (4d6).
acid-splash.name: Acid Splash

aid.description: Choose up to three creatures within range. Each target's hit point maximum and current hit points increase by 5 for the duration.
aid.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, a target's hit points increase by an additional 5 for each slot level above 2nd.
aid.name: Aid

aid.components.material: A tiny strip of white cloth

alarm.description: You set an alarm against unwanted intrusion. Choose a door, a window, or an area within range that is no larger than a 20-foot cube. Until the spell ends, an alarm alerts you whenever a Tiny or larger creature touches or enters the warded area. When you cast the spell, you can designate creatures that won't set off the alarm. You also choose whether the alarm is mental or audible. An audible alarm produces the sound of a hand bell for 10 seconds within 60 feet. A mental alarm alerts you with a ping in your mind if you are within 1 mile of the warded area.
alarm.name: Alarm

alarm.components.material: A tiny bell and a piece of fine silver wire

alter-self.description: You assume a different form. When you cast the spell, choose one of the following options: Aquatic Adaptation (breathing underwater and swim speed), Change Appearance (alter your physical appearance), or Natural Weapons (grow claws, fangs, or similar that deal 1d6 + your modifier).
alter-self.name: Alter Self

animal-friendship.description: This spell lets you convince a beast that you mean it no harm. Choose a beast that you can see within range. It must see and hear you. If the beast's Intelligence is 4 or higher, the spell fails. Otherwise, the beast must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for the spell's duration.
animal-friendship.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can affect one additional beast for each slot level above 1st.
animal-friendship.name: Animal Friendship

animal-messenger.description: By means of this spell, you use an animal to deliver a message. Choose a Tiny beast you can see within range. You specify a location and a recipient who matches a general description, and you speak a message of up to twenty-five words. The beast travels for the duration toward the specified location, covering about 50 miles per 24 hours or 25 miles for a flying messenger.
animal-messenger.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the duration increases by 48 hours for each slot level above 2nd.
animal-messenger.name: Animal Messenger

animal-shapes.description: Your magic turns others into beasts. Choose any number of willing creatures within range. You transform each target into the form of a Large or smaller beast with a challenge rating of 4 or lower. On subsequent turns, you can use your action to transform affected creatures into new forms. The transformation lasts for the duration or until a target drops to 0 hit points or dies.
animal-shapes.name: Animal Shapes

animate-dead.description: You create an undead servant. Choose a pile of bones or a corpse of a Medium or Small Humanoid within range. Your spell imbues the target with a foul mimicry of life, raising it as an Undead creature. The target becomes a Skeleton if you chose bones or a Zombie if you chose a corpse. The creature is under your control for 24 hours, after which it stops obeying any commands. You must cast this spell again to maintain control.
animate-dead.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, you animate or reassert control over two additional Undead creatures for each slot level above 3rd.
animate-dead.name: Animate Dead

animate-dead.components.material: A drop of blood, a piece of flesh, and a pinch of bone dust

animate-objects.description: Objects come to life at your command. Choose up to ten nonmagical objects within range that are not being worn or carried. Medium targets count as two objects, Large targets count as four objects, Huge targets count as eight objects. Each target animates and becomes a creature under your control until the spell ends or until reduced to 0 hit points. As a Bonus Action, you can mentally command any creature you made with this spell within 500 feet of you.
animate-objects.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, you can animate two additional objects for each slot level above 5th.
animate-objects.name: Animate Objects

antilife-shell.description: A shimmering barrier extends out from you in a 10-foot radius and moves with you, remaining centered on you and hedging out creatures other than Undead and Constructs. A creature can't willingly enter the barrier by nonmagical means. If a creature tries to use a teleport or interplanar travel to enter the barrier, it must first succeed on a Charisma saving throw.
antilife-shell.name: Antilife Shell

antimagic-field.description: A 10-foot-radius invisible sphere of antimagic surrounds you. Within this area, spells can't be cast, summoned creatures disappear, and even magic items become mundane. Spells and other magical effects are suppressed in the sphere and can't protrude into it. Slots expended to cast a suppressed spell are still consumed.
antimagic-field.name: Antimagic Field

antimagic-field.components.material: A pinch of powdered iron or iron filings

antipathy-sympathy.description: This spell attracts or repels creatures of your choice. You target something within range, either a Huge or smaller object or creature or an area that is no larger than a 200-foot Cube. Then specify a kind of intelligent creature. You invest the target with an aura that either attracts or repels the specified creatures for the duration. Choose Antipathy (creatures must succeed on a Wisdom save or become Frightened and must move away) or Sympathy (creatures must succeed on a Wisdom save or use movement to move toward the object on each turn).
antipathy-sympathy.name: Antipathy/Sympathy

antipathy-sympathy.components.material: A lump of alum soaked in vinegar (Antipathy) or a drop of honey (Sympathy)

arcane-eye.description: You create an invisible, magical eye within range that hovers in the air for the duration. You mentally receive visual information from the eye, which has normal vision and Darkvision with a range of 30 feet. The eye can look in every direction. As a Magic action, you can move the eye up to 30 feet in any direction. The eye can pass through openings as small as 1 inch in diameter.
arcane-eye.name: Arcane Eye

arcane-lock.description: You touch a closed door, window, gate, chest, or other entryway, and it becomes locked for the duration. You and any creatures you designate can open the object normally. The DC to break or pick the lock increases by 10.
arcane-lock.name: Arcane Lock

arcane-lock.components.material: Gold dust worth at least 25 gp, which the spell consumes

astral-projection.description: You and up to eight willing creatures within range project your astral bodies into the Astral Plane. Your physical body remains behind in a state of suspended animation. Your astral form resembles your mortal form with a silvery cord trailing behind it, connecting it to your material body. If the cord is cut, your soul and body are separated and you die instantly.
astral-projection.name: Astral Projection

astral-projection.components.material: For each creature you affect, one jacinth worth at least 1,000 gp and one ornately carved bar of silver worth at least 100 gp, all of which the spell consumes

augury.description: You receive an omen from an otherworldly entity about the results of a specific course of action that you plan to take within the next 30 minutes. The DM chooses from: Weal (good results), Woe (bad results), Weal and Woe, or Nothing (for actions without meaningful consequences).
augury.name: Augury

augury.components.material: Specially marked sticks, bones, or similar tokens worth at least 25 gp

aura-of-life.description: An aura radiates from you in a 30-foot emanation for the duration. While in the aura, you and your allies have Resistance to Necrotic damage, and their Hit Point maximums can't be reduced. Also, a nonhostile creature with 0 Hit Points that starts its turn in the aura regains 1 Hit Point.
aura-of-life.name: Aura of Life

awaken.description: After spending the casting time tracing magical pathways within a precious gemstone, you touch a Huge or smaller Beast or plant. The target must have either no Intelligence score or an Intelligence of 3 or less. The target gains an Intelligence of 10 and the ability to speak one language you know. A Beast becomes a creature of your choice: it retains its stats but gains sentience. An awakened plant gains the ability to move its limbs and becomes a creature. The awakened creature is charmed by you for 30 days or until you or your companions harm it.
awaken.name: Awaken

awaken.components.material: An agate worth at least 1,000 gp, which the spell consumes

bane.description: Up to three creatures of your choice that you can see within range must make Charisma saving throws. Whenever a target that fails this saving throw makes an attack roll or a saving throw before the spell ends, the target must roll a d4 and subtract the number rolled from the attack roll or saving throw.
bane.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 1st.
bane.name: Bane

banishment.description: You attempt to send one creature that you can see within range to another plane of existence. The target must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be banished. If the target is native to the plane you're on, it is banished to a harmless demiplane and is Incapacitated there. If the target is native to a different plane, it is banished to its home plane. If the spell ends before 1 minute has passed, the target reappears in the space it left.
banishment.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 4th.
banishment.name: Banishment

banishment.components.material: An item distasteful to the target

barkskin.description: You touch a willing creature. Until the spell ends, the target's skin has a rough, bark-like appearance, and the target's AC can't be less than 17, regardless of what kind of armor it is wearing.
barkskin.name: Barkskin

beacon-of-hope.description: You bestow hope upon creatures of your choice within range. For the duration, each target has Advantage on Wisdom saving throws and death saving throws, and regains the maximum number of Hit Points possible from any healing.
beacon-of-hope.name: Beacon of Hope

befuddlement.description: You blast the mind of a creature that you can see within range. The target must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 10d12 Psychic damage and can't cast spells or take the Magic action. At the end of every 30 days, the target can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on a success. The effect can also be ended by the Greater Restoration, Heal, or Wish spell.
befuddlement.name: Befuddlement

befuddlement.components.material: A handful of clay, crystal, glass, or mineral spheres

bestow-curse.description: You touch a creature, and that creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become cursed for the duration. Choose one of the following curses: the target has Disadvantage on ability checks and saving throws made with one ability score of your choice; the target has Disadvantage on attack rolls against you; the target must make a Wisdom saving throw at the start of each of its turns or waste its action that turn; or your attacks and spells deal an extra 1d8 Necrotic damage to the target.
bestow-curse.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the duration is Concentration, up to 10 minutes. With a 5th-level slot, the duration is 8 hours. With a 7th-level slot, the duration is 24 hours. With a 9th-level slot, the spell lasts until dispelled.
bestow-curse.name: Bestow Curse

bigbys-hand.description: You create a Large hand of shimmering, translucent force in an unoccupied space within range. The hand lasts for the duration, moves at your command, and mimics the movements of your own hand. The hand is an object that has AC 20, HP equal to your Hit Point maximum, and a Strength of 26. It doesn't occupy its space. You can command the hand to perform: Clenched Fist (melee spell attack, 4d8 Force damage), Forceful Hand (push creature 5 feet + 5 × Str mod), Grasping Hand (grapple), or Interposing Hand (half cover).
bigbys-hand.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the damage of the Clenched Fist increases by 2d8 for each slot level above 5th.
bigbys-hand.name: Arcane Hand

bigbys-hand.components.material: An eggshell and a snakeskin glove

blade-barrier.description: You create a vertical wall of whirling, razor-sharp blades made of magical energy. The wall appears within range and lasts for the duration. A creature takes 6d10 slashing damage when it enters the wall's area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there. A creature can make a Dexterity saving throw to take half damage.
blade-barrier.name: Blade Barrier

bless.description: You bless up to three creatures of your choice within range. Whenever a target makes an attack roll or a saving throw before the spell ends, the target can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the attack roll or saving throw.
bless.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 1st.
bless.name: Bless

blight.description: Necromantic energy washes over a creature of your choice that you can see within range, draining moisture and vitality from it. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. The target takes 8d8 Necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. This spell has no effect on Constructs or Undead. A Plant creature has Disadvantage on the saving throw.
blight.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 4th.
blight.name: Blight

blindness-deafness.description: You can blind or deafen a foe. Choose one creature that you can see within range to make a Constitution saving throw. If it fails, the target is either blinded or deafened (your choice) for the duration. At the end of each of its turns, the target can make a Constitution saving throw, ending the effect on a success.
blindness-deafness.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 2nd.
blindness-deafness.name: Blindness/Deafness

blink.description: Roll a d20 at the end of each of your turns for the duration of the spell. On a roll of 11 or higher, you vanish from your current plane of existence and appear in the Ethereal Plane. At the start of your next turn, you return to an unoccupied space of your choice within 10 feet of the space you vanished from.
blink.name: Blink

blur.description: Your body becomes blurred, shifting and wavering to all who can see you. For the duration, any creature has disadvantage on attack rolls against you. An attacker is immune to this effect if it doesn't rely on sight or can see through illusions.
blur.name: Blur

burning-hands.description: As you hold your hands with thumbs touching and fingers spread, a thin sheet of flames shoots forth from your outstretched fingertips. Each creature in a 15-foot cone must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes 3d6 fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
burning-hands.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 1st.
burning-hands.name: Burning Hands

call-lightning.description: A storm cloud appears at a point you can see within range, in the shape of a cylinder 10 feet tall with a 60-foot radius. When you cast the spell and as a Magic action on subsequent turns, you can call down a bolt of lightning targeting a point beneath the cloud. Each creature within 5 feet of that point must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 3d10 Lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.
call-lightning.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d10 for each slot level above 3rd.
call-lightning.name: Call Lightning

calm-emotions.description: You attempt to suppress strong emotions in a group of people. Each humanoid in a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on a point you choose within range must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, you can suppress any effect causing a target to be charmed or frightened, or make a target indifferent about hostile creatures.
calm-emotions.name: Calm Emotions

chain-lightning.description: You create a bolt of lightning that arcs toward a target of your choice within range. Three bolts then leap from that target to as many as three other targets, each of which must be within 30 feet of the first target. Each target must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 10d8 lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.
chain-lightning.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, one additional bolt leaps from the first target to another target for each slot level above 6th.
chain-lightning.name: Chain Lightning

chain-lightning.components.material: A bit of fur, a piece of amber, and three silver pins

charm-monster.description: You attempt to charm a creature you can see within range. It must make a Wisdom saving throw, and it does so with Advantage if you or your companions are fighting it. On a failed save, the creature has the Charmed condition until the spell ends or until you or your companions do anything harmful to it. The Charmed creature is friendly to you. When the spell ends, the creature knows it was charmed by you.
charm-monster.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 4th.
charm-monster.name: Charm Monster

charm-person.description: You attempt to charm a humanoid you can see within range. It must make a Wisdom saving throw, and does so with advantage if you or your companions are fighting it. If it fails the saving throw, it is charmed by you until the spell ends or until you or your companions do anything harmful to it. The charmed creature regards you as a friendly acquaintance.
charm-person.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 1st.
charm-person.name: Charm Person

chill-touch.description: You channel necrotic energy through your touch. Make a melee spell attack against a creature within your reach. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 necrotic damage, and it can't regain hit points until the start of your next turn.
chill-touch.higherLevels: The spell's damage increases by 1d10 when you reach 5th level (2d10), 11th level (3d10), and 17th level (4d10).
chill-touch.name: Chill Touch

chromatic-orb.description: You hurl an orb of energy at a creature you can see within range. You choose Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder for the type of orb you create, and then make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 3d8 damage of the type you chose.
chromatic-orb.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 1st.
chromatic-orb.name: Chromatic Orb

chromatic-orb.components.material: A diamond worth at least 50 gp

circle-of-death.description: A sphere of negative energy ripples out in a 60-foot-radius sphere from a point within range. Each creature in that area must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 8d6 necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.
circle-of-death.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, the damage increases by 2d6 for each slot level above 6th.
circle-of-death.name: Circle of Death

circle-of-death.components.material: The powder of a crushed black pearl worth at least 500 gp

clairvoyance.description: You create an invisible sensor within range in a location familiar to you or in an obvious location. The sensor remains for the duration, and you can use it to see or hear (your choice when you cast the spell) as if you were in its space.
clairvoyance.name: Clairvoyance

clairvoyance.components.material: A focus worth 100+ GP, either a jeweled horn for hearing or a glass eye for seeing

clone.description: This spell grows an inert duplicate of a living creature as a safeguard against death. The clone matures after 120 days; at any time after it matures, if the original creature dies, its soul transfers to the clone. The clone is physically identical to the original and has the same personality, memories, and abilities.
clone.name: Clone

clone.components.material: A diamond worth at least 1,000 gp and a sealable vessel worth at least 2,000 gp that is large enough to hold a Medium creature, plus at least 1 cubic inch of flesh of the creature to be cloned

cloudkill.description: You create a 20-foot-radius Sphere of poisonous, yellow-green fog centered on a point you choose within range. The fog spreads around corners. It lasts for the duration or until strong wind disperses it. Its area is Heavily Obscured. When a creature enters the spell's area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there, that creature must make a Constitution saving throw. The creature takes 5d8 Poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The fog moves 10 feet away from you at the start of each of your turns.
cloudkill.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 5th.
cloudkill.name: Cloudkill

color-spray.description: A dazzling array of flashing, colored light springs from your hand. Each creature in a 15-foot Cone must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have the Blinded condition until the end of your next turn.
color-spray.name: Color Spray

color-spray.components.material: A pinch of powder or sand colored red, yellow, and blue

command.description: You speak a one-word command to a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or follow the command on its next turn. The spell has no effect if the target is undead, if it doesn't understand your language, or if your command is directly harmful to it. Common commands include Approach, Drop, Flee, Grovel, and Halt.
command.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can affect one additional creature for each slot level above 1st.
command.name: Command

commune.description: You contact your deity or a divine proxy and ask up to three questions that can be answered with a yes or no. You must ask your questions before the spell ends. You receive a correct answer for each question. Divine beings aren't necessarily omniscient, so you might receive 'unclear' as an answer if the question pertains to information beyond the deity's knowledge.
commune.name: Commune

commune-with-nature.description: You briefly become one with nature and gain knowledge of the surrounding territory. In the outdoors, the spell gives you knowledge of the land within 3 miles of you. In caves and other natural underground settings, the radius is limited to 300 feet. You gain knowledge of up to three of the following: terrain and bodies of water, prevalent plants, minerals, animals, or peoples, powerful celestials, fey, fiends, elementals, or undead, or influence from other planes.
commune-with-nature.name: Commune with Nature

commune.components.material: Incense and a vial of holy or unholy water

comprehend-languages.description: For the duration, you understand the literal meaning of any spoken language that you hear. You also understand any written language that you see, but you must be touching the surface on which the words are written. It takes about 1 minute to read one page of text. This spell doesn't decode secret messages or glyphs that aren't part of a written language.
comprehend-languages.name: Comprehend Languages

comprehend-languages.components.material: A pinch of soot and salt

compulsion.description: Each creature of your choice that you can see within range must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a target is affected by this spell. Until the spell ends, on each of your turns you can use a Bonus Action to designate a direction that is horizontal to you. Each affected target must use as much of its movement as possible to move in that direction on its next turn, without moving into an obviously deadly hazard.
compulsion.name: Compulsion

cone-of-cold.description: A blast of cold air erupts from your hands. Each creature in a 60-foot Cone originating from you must make a Constitution saving throw. A creature takes 8d8 Cold damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature killed by this spell becomes a frozen statue until it thaws.
cone-of-cold.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 5th.
cone-of-cold.name: Cone of Cold

cone-of-cold.components.material: A small crystal or glass cone

confusion.description: Each creature in a 10-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point you choose within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be affected. An affected target can't take Reactions and must roll a d10 at the start of each of its turns to determine its behavior: 1 (move randomly), 2-6 (no action or movement), 7-8 (melee attack random adjacent creature), 9-10 (acts normally). At the end of each turn, an affected target can repeat the saving throw.
confusion.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the radius of the Sphere increases by 5 feet for each slot level above 4th.
confusion.name: Confusion

conjure-animals.description: You conjure nature spirits that appear as a Large pack of spectral animals in a 15-foot Emanation originating from a point you choose within range. Each creature in that area when the spirits appear must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 3d10 Slashing damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one. As a Bonus Action on subsequent turns, you can move the Emanation up to 30 feet, and each creature that enters the area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there must repeat the save.
conjure-animals.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d10 for each slot level above 3rd.
conjure-animals.name: Conjure Animals

conjure-celestial.description: You conjure a spirit from the Upper Planes that manifests as a pillar of light in a 10-foot-radius, 40-foot-high Cylinder centered on a point within range. For each creature you can see in the Cylinder, choose which light shines on it: Healing Light (the target regains Hit Points equal to 4d12 plus your spellcasting ability modifier) or Searing Light (the target makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 6d12 Radiant damage on a failed save or half as much on a success).
conjure-celestial.higherLevels: The healing and the damage increase by 1d12 for each spell slot level above 7.
conjure-celestial.name: Conjure Celestial

conjure-elemental.description: You conjure a Large, intangible spirit from the Elemental Planes in an unoccupied space within range. Choose the spirit's element, which sets its damage type: air (Lightning), earth (Thunder), fire (Fire), or water (Cold). Whenever a creature you can see enters the spirit's space or starts its turn within 5 feet of it, and the spirit has no creature Restrained, you can force that creature to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 8d8 damage of the spirit's type and has the Restrained condition until the spell ends; on a success it takes half damage.
conjure-elemental.higherLevels: The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 5.
conjure-elemental.name: Conjure Elemental

conjure-fey.description: You summon a fey creature of challenge rating 6 or lower, or a fey spirit that takes the form of a beast of challenge rating 6 or lower. The creature appears in an unoccupied space within range and is friendly to you and your companions. It obeys your verbal commands and acts on your turn in combat.
conjure-fey.name: Conjure Fey

conjure-minor-elementals.description: You conjure elemental spirits that swirl around you in a 15-foot emanation for the duration. Until the spell ends, any attack you make deals an extra 2d8 damage of a type you choose when you cast the spell: Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder.
conjure-minor-elementals.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the extra damage increases by 2d8 for each slot level above 4th.
conjure-minor-elementals.name: Conjure Minor Elementals

conjure-woodland-beings.description: You conjure nature spirits that flit around you in a 10-foot emanation for the duration. Whenever the emanation enters the space of a creature you can see and whenever a creature you can see enters the emanation or ends its turn there, you can force that creature to make a Wisdom saving throw. The creature takes 5d8 Force damage on a failed save or half as much on a successful one. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
conjure-woodland-beings.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 4th.
conjure-woodland-beings.name: Conjure Woodland Beings

contact-other-plane.description: You mentally contact a demigod, the spirit of a long-dead sage, or some other mysterious entity from another plane. Contacting this extraplanar intelligence can strain or even break your mind. When you cast this spell, make a DC 15 Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, you take 6d6 Psychic damage and are insane until you finish a Long Rest. While insane, you can't take actions, can't understand what other creatures say, can't read, and speak only in gibberish. You can ask the entity up to five questions. The entity answers each question with one word: yes, no, maybe, never, irrelevant, or unclear.
contact-other-plane.name: Contact Other Plane

contagion.description: Your touch inflicts disease. Make a melee spell attack against a creature within your reach. On a hit, the target contracts a natural disease of your choice from the options below. At the end of each of the target's turns, it must make a Constitution saving throw. After failing three of these saving throws, the disease's effects last for the duration, and the creature stops making saves. After succeeding on three, the creature recovers. Diseases include: Blinding Sickness, Filth Fever, Flesh Rot, Mindfire, Seizure, and Slimy Doom.
contagion.name: Contagion

contingency.description: Choose a spell of 5th level or lower that you can cast, that has a casting time of an action, and that can target you. You cast that spell—called the contingent spell—as part of casting Contingency, expending spell slots for both. The contingent spell doesn't come into effect until a certain circumstance occurs. You describe that circumstance when you cast the two spells. The contingent spell takes effect immediately after the circumstance is met for the first time.
contingency.name: Contingency

contingency.components.material: A statuette of yourself carved from ivory and decorated with gems worth at least 1,500 gp

continual-flame.description: A flame, equivalent in brightness to a torch, springs forth from an object that you touch. The effect looks like a regular flame, but it creates no heat and doesn't use oxygen. A continual flame can be covered or hidden but not smothered or quenched.
continual-flame.name: Continual Flame

continual-flame.components.material: Ruby dust worth 50 gp, which the spell consumes

control-water.description: Until the spell ends, you control any water inside an area you choose that is a Cube up to 100 feet on a side. Choose one of the following effects: Flood (raise water level up to 20 feet), Part Water (create a trench), Redirect Flow (cause flowing water to move in a direction you choose), or Whirlpool (form a vortex that deals damage and restrains).
control-water.name: Control Water

control-water.components.material: A drop of water and a pinch of dust

control-weather.description: You take control of the weather within 5 miles of you for the duration. You must be outdoors to cast this spell. You can change precipitation, temperature, and wind conditions. It takes 1d4 × 10 minutes for the new conditions to take effect. You can change the conditions again at any time, and the new conditions take effect after the same delay.
control-weather.name: Control Weather

control-weather.components.material: Burning incense and bits of earth and wood mixed in water

counterspell.description: You attempt to interrupt a creature in the process of casting a spell. The creature makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the spell dissipates with no effect, and the action, Bonus Action, or Reaction used to cast it is wasted. If that spell was cast with a spell slot, the slot isn't expended.
counterspell.name: Counterspell

create-food-and-water.description: You create 45 pounds of food and 30 gallons of fresh water on the ground or in containers within range, enough to sustain up to fifteen Humanoids or five steeds for 24 hours. The food is bland but nourishing and spoils if uneaten after 24 hours. The water is clean and doesn't go bad.
create-food-and-water.name: Create Food and Water

create-or-destroy-water.description: You either create or destroy water. Create Water: You create up to 10 gallons of clean water within range in an open container, or the water falls as rain in a 30-foot cube within range. Destroy Water: You destroy up to 10 gallons of water in an open container within range, or you destroy fog in a 30-foot cube within range.
create-or-destroy-water.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you create or destroy 10 additional gallons of water, or the size of the cube increases by 5 feet, for each slot level above 1st.
create-or-destroy-water.name: Create or Destroy Water

create-or-destroy-water.components.material: A drop of water if creating water or a few grains of sand if destroying it

create-undead.description: You can cast this spell only at night. Choose up to three corpses of Medium or Small humanoids within range. Each corpse becomes a ghoul under your control. The creatures obey your mental commands and remain under your control for 24 hours, after which they stop obeying unless you cast this spell again.
create-undead.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a 7th-level slot, you can create or reassert control over four ghouls. With an 8th-level slot, you can create ghasts or wights. With a 9th-level slot, you can create mummies.
create-undead.name: Create Undead

create-undead.components.material: One clay pot filled with grave dirt and one black onyx stone worth at least 150 gp for each corpse

creation.description: You pull wisps of shadow material from the Shadowfell to create a nonliving object of vegetable matter within range: soft goods, rope, wood, or something similar. You can also use this spell to create mineral objects such as stone, crystal, or metal. The object created must be no larger than a 5-foot cube. The duration depends on the object's material: Vegetable matter lasts 1 day, Stone or crystal lasts 12 hours, Precious metals last 1 hour, Gems last 10 minutes, Adamantine or mithral lasts 1 minute.
creation.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the cube increases by 5 feet for each slot level above 5th.
creation.name: Creation

creation.components.material: A tiny piece of matter of the same type as the item you plan to create

cure-wounds.description: A creature you touch regains a number of hit points equal to 2d8 + your spellcasting ability modifier. This spell has no effect on undead or constructs.
cure-wounds.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the healing increases by 2d8 for each slot level above 1st.
cure-wounds.name: Cure Wounds

dancing-lights.description: You create up to four torch-sized lights within range, making them appear as torches, lanterns, or glowing orbs that hover in the air for the duration. Each light sheds dim light in a 10-foot radius. As a Bonus Action, you can move the lights up to 60 feet to a new spot within range.
dancing-lights.name: Dancing Lights

darkness.description: Magical darkness spreads from a point you choose within range to fill a 15-foot-radius sphere for the duration. Darkvision can't penetrate this darkness. Nonmagical light can't illuminate it. If any of the spell's area overlaps with light created by a spell of 2nd level or lower, the spell creating the light is dispelled.
darkness.name: Darkness

darkness.components.material: Bat fur and a drop of pitch or piece of coal

darkvision.description: You touch a willing creature to grant it the ability to see in the dark. For the duration, that creature has darkvision out to a range of 150 feet.
darkvision.name: Darkvision

darkvision.components.material: Either a pinch of dried carrot or an agate

daylight.description: A 60-foot-radius sphere of Bright Light springs into existence at a point you choose within range. The sphere is Dim Light for an additional 60 feet. If you chose a point on an object you are holding or one that isn't being worn or carried, the light shines from the object and moves with it. Completely covering the affected object with an opaque object blocks the light.
daylight.name: Daylight

death-ward.description: You touch a creature and grant it a measure of protection from death. The first time the target would drop to 0 Hit Points before the spell ends, the target instead drops to 1 Hit Point, and the spell ends. If the spell is still in effect when the target is subjected to an effect that would kill it instantaneously without dealing damage, that effect is negated against the target, and the spell ends.
death-ward.name: Death Ward

delayed-blast-fireball.description: A beam of yellow light flashes from your pointing finger and condenses at a chosen point within range as a glowing bead for the duration. When the spell ends or a creature touches the bead, it detonates in a 20-foot-radius sphere. Each creature in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 12d6 fire damage on a failed save, or half on a success. The damage increases by 1d6 for each round the bead remains before detonating.
delayed-blast-fireball.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 8th level or higher, the base damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 7th.
delayed-blast-fireball.name: Delayed Blast Fireball

delayed-blast-fireball.components.material: A tiny ball of bat guano and sulfur

demiplane.description: You create a shadowy door on a flat solid surface that you can see within range. The door is large enough to allow Medium creatures to pass through unhindered. When opened, the door leads to a demiplane that appears to be an empty room 30 feet in each dimension, made of wood or stone. When the spell ends, the door disappears, and any creatures or objects inside the demiplane remain trapped there. Each time you cast this spell, you can create a new demiplane or connect to one you previously created.
demiplane.name: Demiplane

detect-evil-and-good.description: For the duration, you know if there is an aberration, celestial, elemental, fey, fiend, or undead within 30 feet of you, as well as where the creature is located. Similarly, you know if there is a place or object within 30 feet of you that has been magically consecrated or desecrated.
detect-evil-and-good.name: Detect Evil and Good

detect-magic.description: For the duration, you sense the presence of magic within 30 feet of you. If you sense magic in this way, you can use your action to see a faint aura around any visible creature or object in the area that bears magic, and you learn its school of magic, if any.
detect-magic.name: Detect Magic

detect-poison-and-disease.description: For the duration, you can sense the presence and location of poisons, poisonous creatures, and diseases within 30 feet of you. You also identify the kind of poison, poisonous creature, or disease in each case.
detect-poison-and-disease.name: Detect Poison and Disease

detect-thoughts.description: For the duration, you can read the thoughts of certain creatures. You initially detect the presence of thinking creatures within 30 feet. You can focus on one creature to learn its surface thoughts. The target can make a Wisdom saving throw to resist deeper probing.
detect-thoughts.name: Detect Thoughts

dimension-door.description: You teleport yourself from your current location to any other spot within range. You arrive at exactly the spot desired. It can be a place you can see, one you can visualize, or one you can describe by stating distance and direction. You can bring along objects as long as their weight doesn't exceed what you can carry. You can also bring one willing creature of your size or smaller that is carrying gear up to its carrying capacity.
dimension-door.name: Dimension Door

disguise-self.description: You make yourself—including your clothing, armor, weapons, and other belongings on your person—look different until the spell ends or until you use your action to dismiss it. You can seem up to 1 foot shorter or taller and can appear thin, fat, or in between. You can't change your body type, so you must adopt a form that has the same basic arrangement of limbs. The changes wrought by this spell fail to hold up to physical inspection.
disguise-self.name: Disguise Self

disintegrate.description: A thin green ray springs from your pointing finger to a target within range. The target can be a creature, an object, or a creation of magical force. A creature targeted by this spell must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 10d6 + 40 force damage on a failed save. If this damage reduces the target to 0 hit points, it is disintegrated.
disintegrate.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, the damage increases by 3d6 for each slot level above 6th.
disintegrate.name: Disintegrate

disintegrate.components.material: A lodestone and a pinch of dust

dispel-evil-and-good.description: Shimmering energy surrounds and protects you from Aberrations, Celestials, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, and Undead. Creatures of those types have Disadvantage on attack rolls against you. You can end the spell early by using either of the following special functions: Break Enchantment (touch a creature Charmed, Frightened, or Possessed by such a creature to end that effect) or Dismissal (make a melee spell attack against such a creature, forcing it to make a Charisma save or be sent to its home plane).
dispel-evil-and-good.name: Dispel Evil and Good

dispel-evil-and-good.components.material: Holy water or powdered silver and iron

dispel-magic.description: You choose one creature, object, or magical effect within range. Any spell of 3rd level or lower on the target ends. For each spell of 4th level or higher on the target, make an ability check using your spellcasting ability (DC 10 + the spell's level). On a successful check, the spell ends.
dispel-magic.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, you automatically end the effects of a spell on the target if the spell's level is equal to or less than the level of the spell slot you used.
dispel-magic.name: Dispel Magic

dissonant-whispers.description: You whisper a discordant melody that only one creature of your choice within range can hear, wracking it with terrible pain. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 3d6 psychic damage and must immediately use its reaction, if available, to move as far as its speed allows away from you. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage and doesn't have to move away.
dissonant-whispers.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 1st.
dissonant-whispers.name: Dissonant Whispers

divination.description: Your magic and an offering put you in contact with a god or a god's servants. You ask a single question concerning a specific goal, event, or activity to occur within 7 days. The DM offers a truthful reply, which might be a short phrase, a cryptic rhyme, or an omen.
divination.name: Divination

divination.components.material: Incense and a sacrificial offering appropriate to your religion, together worth at least 25 gp, which the spell consumes

divine-favor.description: Your prayer empowers you with divine radiance. Until the spell ends, your weapon attacks deal an extra 1d4 radiant damage on a hit.
divine-favor.name: Divine Favor

divine-smite.description: You channel divine energy into a strike. You deal an extra 2d8 radiant damage to the target. The damage increases by 1d8 if the target is an undead or a fiend.
divine-smite.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the extra damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 1st.
divine-smite.name: Divine Smite

divine-word.description: You utter a divine word, imbued with the power that shaped the world. Each creature of your choice within range that can hear you must make a Charisma saving throw. Depending on remaining hit points, a creature suffers different effects: 50 HP or fewer is deafened for 1 minute, 40 or fewer is blinded and deafened, 30 or fewer is stunned, blinded, and deafened, 20 or fewer is killed instantly.
divine-word.name: Divine Word

dominate-beast.description: You attempt to beguile a Beast that you can see within range. It must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Charmed condition for the duration. The Charmed target obeys your telepathic commands. Each time the target takes damage, it makes a new Wisdom saving throw against the spell. If the saving throw succeeds, the spell ends.
dominate-beast.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a 5th-level spell slot, the duration is Concentration, up to 10 minutes. When you use a 6th-level slot, the duration is Concentration, up to 1 hour. When you use a slot of 7th level or higher, the duration is Concentration, up to 8 hours.
dominate-beast.name: Dominate Beast

dominate-monster.description: You attempt to beguile a creature within range. It must make a Wisdom saving throw, and it does so with advantage if you or your companions are fighting it. On a failure, it is charmed by you for the duration. While charmed, you have a telepathic link with it and can issue commands as a bonus action, which it does its best to obey.
dominate-monster.higherLevels: When you cast this spell with a 9th-level spell slot, the duration is concentration, up to 8 hours.
dominate-monster.name: Dominate Monster

dominate-person.description: You attempt to beguile a Humanoid that you can see within range. It must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be Charmed by you for the duration. The Charmed target is under your telepathic control. Each time the target takes damage, it makes a new Wisdom saving throw against the spell. On a success, the spell ends. You can use your action to take total and precise control of the target.
dominate-person.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a 6th-level spell slot, the duration is Concentration, up to 10 minutes. With a 7th-level slot, up to 1 hour. With an 8th-level or higher slot, up to 8 hours.
dominate-person.name: Dominate Person

dragons-breath.description: You touch one willing creature and imbue it with the power to spew magical energy from its mouth. Choose Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison. Until the spell ends, the creature can use an action to exhale energy of the chosen type in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 3d6 damage of the chosen type on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.
dragons-breath.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 2nd.
dragons-breath.name: Dragon's Breath

drawmijs-instant-summons.description: You touch an object weighing 10 pounds or less whose longest dimension is 6 feet or less. The spell leaves an invisible mark on its surface and invisibly inscribes the name of the item on the sapphire you use as the material component. Each time you cast this spell, you must use a different sapphire. At any time thereafter, you can use your action to speak the item's name and crush the sapphire. The item instantly appears in your hand.
drawmijs-instant-summons.name: Instant Summons

drawmijs-instant-summons.components.material: A sapphire worth at least 1,000 gp

dream.description: This spell shapes a creature's dreams. Choose a creature known to you as the target of this spell. The target must be on the same plane of existence as you. You, or a willing creature you touch, enters a trance state, acting as a messenger. While in the trance, the messenger is aware of their surroundings but can't take actions or move. If the target is asleep, the messenger appears in the target's dreams and can converse with the target. The messenger can also shape the environment of the dream. If the target is awake when you cast the spell, the messenger knows it, and can either end the trance or wait for the target to fall asleep.
dream.name: Dream

dream.components.material: A handful of sand, a dab of ink, and a writing quill plucked from a sleeping bird

druidcraft.description: Whispering to the spirits of nature, you create one of the following effects within range: you create a tiny, harmless sensory effect that predicts the weather; you instantly make a flower blossom, a seed pod open, or a leaf bud bloom; you create an instantaneous, harmless sensory effect such as falling leaves or a puff of wind; or you instantly light or snuff out a candle, torch, or small campfire.
druidcraft.name: Druidcraft

earthquake.description: You create a seismic disturbance at a point on the ground within range. For the duration, an intense tremor rips through the ground in a 100-foot-radius circle. Each creature on the ground in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw or be knocked prone. Structures in contact with the ground take 50 bludgeoning damage at the start of each turn and may collapse.
earthquake.name: Earthquake

earthquake.components.material: A pinch of dirt, a piece of rock, and a lump of clay

eldritch-blast.description: A beam of crackling energy streaks toward a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 force damage.
eldritch-blast.higherLevels: The spell creates more than one beam when you reach higher levels: two beams at 5th level, three beams at 11th level, and four beams at 17th level. You can direct the beams at the same target or at different ones. Make a separate attack roll for each beam.
eldritch-blast.name: Eldritch Blast

elementalism.description: You exert control over the elements, creating one of the following effects within range: you create a harmless sensory effect related to air, earth, fire, or water; you instantaneously clean or soil an object no larger than 1 cubic foot; you chill, warm, or flavor up to 1 cubic foot of nonliving material; or you cause earth, fire, water, or mist to form into a crude shape for 1 hour.
elementalism.name: Elementalism

enhance-ability.description: You touch a creature and bestow upon it a magical enhancement. Choose one of the following effects: Bear's Endurance, Bull's Strength, Cat's Grace, Eagle's Splendor, Fox's Cunning, or Owl's Wisdom. The target has advantage on ability checks with the chosen ability and gains additional benefits.
enhance-ability.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 2nd.
enhance-ability.name: Enhance Ability

enhance-ability.components.material: Fur or a feather from a beast

enlarge-reduce.description: You cause a creature or an object you can see within range to grow larger or smaller for the duration. An unwilling creature can make a Constitution saving throw to resist. Enlarge doubles size and adds 1d4 to weapon damage. Reduce halves size and subtracts 1d4 from weapon damage.
enlarge-reduce.name: Enlarge/Reduce

ensnaring-strike.description: The next time you hit a creature with a weapon attack before this spell ends, a writhing mass of thorny vines appears at the point of impact, and the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be restrained by the magical vines until the spell ends. While restrained, the target takes 1d6 piercing damage at the start of each of its turns. A creature restrained by the vines can use its action to make a Strength check against your spell save DC, freeing itself on a success.
ensnaring-strike.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 1st.
ensnaring-strike.name: Ensnaring Strike

entangle.description: Grasping weeds and vines sprout from the ground in a 20-foot square starting from a point within range. For the duration, these plants turn the ground in the area into difficult terrain. A creature in the area when you cast the spell must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be restrained by the entangling plants until the spell ends. A restrained creature can use its action to make a Strength check against your spell save DC, freeing itself on a success.
entangle.name: Entangle

enthrall.description: You weave a distracting string of words, causing creatures of your choice that you can see within range and that can hear you to make a Wisdom saving throw. Any creature that can't be charmed succeeds on this saving throw automatically. On a failed save, the target has Disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks made to perceive any creature other than you until the spell ends.
enthrall.name: Enthrall

etherealness.description: You step into the border regions of the Ethereal Plane, in the area where it overlaps with your current plane. You remain in the Border Ethereal for the duration or until you use an action to dismiss the spell. While on the Ethereal Plane, you can see and hear the plane you originated from in shades of gray, but you can't see anything more than 60 feet away.
etherealness.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 8th level or higher, you can target up to three willing creatures (including you) for each slot level above 7th.
etherealness.name: Etherealness

evards-black-tentacles.description: Squirming, ebony tentacles fill a 20-foot square on ground that you can see within range. For the duration, these tentacles turn the ground in the area into Difficult Terrain. When a creature enters the affected area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there, the creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 3d6 Bludgeoning damage and be Restrained by the tentacles until the spell ends. A creature that starts its turn in the area and is already Restrained takes 3d6 Bludgeoning damage. A Restrained creature can use its action to make a Strength or Dexterity check against your spell save DC to free itself.
evards-black-tentacles.name: Black Tentacles

evards-black-tentacles.components.material: A piece of tentacle from a giant octopus or a giant squid

expeditious-retreat.description: This spell allows you to move at an incredible pace. When you cast this spell, and then as a bonus action on each of your turns until the spell ends, you can take the Dash action.
expeditious-retreat.name: Expeditious Retreat

eyebite.description: For the spell's duration, your eyes become an inky void. One creature of your choice within 60 feet must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be affected by one of the following effects of your choice: Asleep (falls unconscious), Panicked (frightened and must Dash away), or Sickened (has disadvantage on attacks and ability checks).
eyebite.name: Eyebite

fabricate.description: You convert raw materials into products of the same material. For example, you can fabricate a wooden bridge from a clump of trees, a rope from a patch of hemp, and clothes from flax or wool. Choose raw materials that you can see within range. You can fabricate a Large or smaller object (contained within a 10-foot Cube) given a sufficient quantity of raw material.
fabricate.name: Fabricate

faerie-fire.description: Each object in a 20-foot cube within range is outlined in blue, green, or violet light (your choice). Any creature in the area when the spell is cast is also outlined in light if it fails a Dexterity saving throw. For the duration, objects and affected creatures shed dim light in a 10-foot radius. Any attack roll against an affected creature or object has advantage if the attacker can see it, and the affected creature or object can't benefit from being invisible.
faerie-fire.name: Faerie Fire

false-life.description: Bolstering yourself with a necromantic facsimile of life, you gain 2d4 + 4 temporary hit points for the duration.
false-life.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you gain 5 additional temporary hit points for each slot level above 1st.
false-life.name: False Life

fear.description: You project a phantasmal image of a creature's worst fears. Each creature in a 30-foot Cone must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or drop whatever it is holding and become Frightened for the duration. While Frightened, a creature must take the Dash action and move away from you by the safest available route on each of its turns. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
fear.name: Fear

fear.components.material: A white feather or the heart of a hen

feather-fall.description: Choose up to five falling creatures within range. A falling creature's rate of descent slows to 60 feet per round until the spell ends. If the creature lands before the spell ends, it takes no falling damage and can land on its feet, and the spell ends for that creature.
feather-fall.name: Feather Fall

feather-fall.components.material: A small feather or piece of down

find-familiar.description: You gain the service of a familiar, a spirit that takes an animal form you choose. The familiar appears in an unoccupied space within range. It acts independently of you but obeys your commands. In combat, it rolls its own initiative and acts on its own turn. It can't attack, but it can take other actions as normal. When the familiar drops to 0 hit points, it disappears. You can communicate telepathically with it while it is within 100 feet, and as an action, you can see through its eyes and hear through its ears.
find-familiar.name: Find Familiar

find-familiar.components.material: Charcoal, incense, and herbs worth 10 gp, consumed

find-steed.description: You summon a spirit that assumes the form of an unusually intelligent, strong, and loyal steed, creating a long-lasting bond with it. The steed takes the form of a warhorse, pony, camel, elk, or mastiff. It serves as a mount and has an Intelligence of at least 6. While mounted on it, you can make any spell you cast that targets only you also target the steed.
find-steed.name: Find Steed

find-the-path.description: This spell allows you to find the shortest, most direct physical route to a specific fixed location that you are familiar with on the same plane of existence. For the duration, as long as you are on the same plane as the destination, you know how far it is and in what direction it lies.
find-the-path.name: Find the Path

find-the-path.components.material: A set of divinatory tools worth 100 gp and an object from the location you wish to find

find-traps.description: You sense the presence of any trap within range that is within line of sight. A trap includes anything that would inflict a sudden or unexpected effect you consider harmful or undesirable. You learn the general nature of each trap's danger but not its exact location.
find-traps.name: Find Traps

finger-of-death.description: You send negative energy coursing through a creature within range, causing it searing pain. The target must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 7d8 + 30 necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. A humanoid killed by this spell rises as a zombie permanently under your command at the start of your next turn.
finger-of-death.name: Finger of Death

fire-bolt.description: You hurl a mote of fire at a creature or object within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 fire damage. A flammable object hit by this spell catches fire if it isn't being worn or carried.
fire-bolt.higherLevels: The spell's damage increases by 1d10 when you reach 5th level (2d10), 11th level (3d10), and 17th level (4d10).
fire-bolt.name: Fire Bolt

fire-shield.description: Flames wreathe your body for the duration, shedding Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet. You can end the spell early by using an action to dismiss it. The flames provide you with a Warm Shield or a Cold Shield, as you choose. The Warm Shield grants you Resistance to Cold damage, and the Cold Shield grants you Resistance to Fire damage. In addition, whenever a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a melee attack, the shield erupts with flame. The attacker takes 2d8 Fire damage from a Warm Shield, or 2d8 Cold damage from a Cold Shield.
fire-shield.name: Fire Shield

fire-shield.components.material: A bit of phosphorus or a firefly

fire-storm.description: A storm made up of sheets of roaring flame appears in a location you choose within range. The area of the storm consists of up to ten 10-foot cubes, which you can arrange as you wish. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 7d10 fire damage on a failed save, or half on a success. You can choose to have the fire leave plant life unharmed.
fire-storm.name: Fire Storm

fireball.description: A bright streak flashes from your pointing finger to a point you choose within range and then blossoms with a low roar into an explosion of flame. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point must make a Dexterity saving throw. A target takes 8d6 Fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The fire spreads around corners and ignites flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried.
fireball.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 3rd.
fireball.name: Fireball

fireball.components.material: A tiny ball of bat guano and sulfur

flame-blade.description: You evoke a fiery blade in your free hand. The blade is similar in size and shape to a scimitar and lasts for the duration. You can use your action to make a melee spell attack with the blade, dealing 3d6 fire damage on a hit. The blade sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet.
flame-blade.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for every two slot levels above 2nd.
flame-blade.name: Flame Blade

flame-strike.description: A vertical column of divine fire roars down from the heavens in a location you specify. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius, 40-foot-high Cylinder centered on a point within range must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes 5d6 Fire damage and 5d6 Radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
flame-strike.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the Fire damage or the Radiant damage (your choice) increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 5th.
flame-strike.name: Flame Strike

flaming-sphere.description: A 5-foot-diameter sphere of fire appears in an unoccupied space of your choice within range. Any creature that ends its turn within 5 feet of the sphere must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 2d6 fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success. As a bonus action, you can move the sphere up to 30 feet.
flaming-sphere.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 2nd.
flaming-sphere.name: Flaming Sphere

flaming-sphere.components.material: A bit of tallow, a pinch of brimite, and a dusting of powdered iron

flesh-to-stone.description: You attempt to turn one creature that you can see within range into stone. If the target's body is made of flesh, the creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it has the Restrained condition as its flesh begins to harden. On a successful save, the creature isn't affected. A Restrained creature must make a Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns. If it successfully saves three times, the spell ends. If it fails three times, it is turned to stone and has the Petrified condition for the duration.
flesh-to-stone.name: Flesh to Stone

flesh-to-stone.components.material: A pinch of lime, water, and earth

fly.description: You touch a willing creature. The target gains a flying speed of 60 feet for the duration. When the spell ends, the target falls if it is still aloft, unless it can stop the fall.
fly.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 3rd.
fly.name: Fly

fly.components.material: A wing feather from any bird

fog-cloud.description: You create a 20-foot-radius sphere of fog centered on a point within range. The sphere spreads around corners, and its area is heavily obscured. It lasts for the duration or until a wind of moderate or greater speed (at least 10 miles per hour) disperses it.
fog-cloud.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the radius of the fog increases by 20 feet for each slot level above 1st.
fog-cloud.name: Fog Cloud

forbiddance.description: You create a ward against magical travel that protects up to 40,000 square feet of floor space to a height of 30 feet above the floor. For the duration, creatures can't teleport into the area or use portals to enter the area. The spell damages types of creatures that you choose when you cast it. Choose one or more of the following: Celestials, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, and Undead. When a chosen creature enters the area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there, it takes 5d10 Radiant or Necrotic damage (your choice when you cast this spell).
forbiddance.name: Forbiddance

forbiddance.components.material: A sprinkling of holy water, rare incense, and powdered ruby worth at least 1,000 gp

forcecage.description: An immobile, Invisible, cube-shaped prison composed of magical force springs into existence around an area you choose within range. The prison can be a cage or a solid box. A creature inside the cage can't leave it by nonmagical means. If the creature tries to use teleportation or interplanar travel to leave, it must first succeed on a Charisma saving throw; on a success it can use that magic to exit the cage. Spells and other magical effects can't extend through the cage or be cast through it.
forcecage.name: Forcecage

foresight.description: You touch a willing creature and bestow a limited ability to see into the immediate future. For the duration, the target can't be surprised and has advantage on attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws. Additionally, other creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls against the target for the duration.
foresight.name: Foresight

freedom-of-movement.description: You touch a willing creature. For the duration, the target's movement is unaffected by Difficult Terrain, and spells and other magical effects can neither reduce the target's speed nor cause the target to have the Paralyzed or Restrained condition. The target can also spend 5 feet of movement to automatically escape from nonmagical restraints.
freedom-of-movement.name: Freedom of Movement

freedom-of-movement.components.material: A leather strap, bound around the arm or a similar appendage

gaseous-form.description: You transform a willing creature you touch, along with everything it's wearing and carrying, into a misty cloud for the duration. The spell ends if the creature drops to 0 Hit Points. The target's only method of movement is a Fly Speed of 10 feet. The target can enter and occupy the space of another creature. The target has Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage and has Advantage on Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution saving throws. The target can pass through small holes, narrow openings, and even mere cracks.
gaseous-form.name: Gaseous Form

gate.description: You conjure a portal linking an unoccupied space you can see within range to a precise location on a different plane of existence. The portal is a circular opening 5 to 20 feet in diameter. Any creature that enters the portal instantly appears within 5 feet of the destination. You can also speak the true name of a specific creature to draw it through the portal.
gate.name: Gate

gate.components.material: A diamond worth at least 5,000 gp

geas.description: You place a magical command on a creature that you can see within range, forcing it to carry out some service or refrain from some action or course of activity as you decide. If the creature can understand you, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become Charmed by you for the duration. While Charmed, the creature takes 5d10 Psychic damage each time it acts in a manner directly counter to your instructions, but no more than once each day. The spell has no effect on a creature that can't understand you or if you command it to do something obviously suicidal.
geas.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a 7th-level spell slot, the duration is 1 year. When you use a 9th-level spell slot, the spell lasts until it is ended by Remove Curse or similar magic.
geas.name: Geas

gentle-repose.description: You touch a corpse or other remains. For the duration, the target is protected from decay and can't become undead. The spell also effectively extends the time limit on raising the target from the dead, since days spent under the influence of this spell don't count against the time limit of spells such as Raise Dead.
gentle-repose.name: Gentle Repose

gentle-repose.components.material: 2 copper pieces, which the spell consumes

giant-insect.description: You summon a giant centipede, spider, wasp, or scorpion, which appears in an unoccupied space you can see within range. The creature is friendly to you and your companions. In combat it acts on your initiative and obeys your verbal commands. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 Hit Points or when the spell ends.
giant-insect.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, you can summon an additional insect for every two slot levels above 4th.
giant-insect.name: Giant Insect

glibness.description: Until the spell ends, when you make a Charisma check, you can replace the number you roll with a 15. Additionally, no matter what you say, magic that would determine if you are telling the truth indicates that you are being truthful.
glibness.name: Glibness

globe-of-invulnerability.description: An immobile, faintly shimmering barrier springs into existence in a 10-foot radius around you and remains for the duration. Any spell of 5th level or lower cast from outside the barrier can't affect creatures or objects within it. Such a spell can target creatures and objects within the barrier, but the spell has no effect on them.
globe-of-invulnerability.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, the barrier blocks spells of one level higher for each slot level above 6th.
globe-of-invulnerability.name: Globe of Invulnerability

globe-of-invulnerability.components.material: A glass or crystal bead that shatters when the spell ends

glyph-of-warding.description: When you cast this spell, you inscribe a harmful glyph either on a surface or within an object that can be closed. If you choose a surface, the glyph can cover an area no larger than 10 feet in diameter. If you choose an object, the object must remain in place; if it moves more than 10 feet from where you cast this spell, the glyph is broken. When triggered, the glyph erupts with magical energy in a 20-foot-radius Sphere. You can set the glyph to deal Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage (5d8) or store a spell of 3rd level or lower.
glyph-of-warding.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by 1d8 for each slot level above 3rd. If you store a spell, you can store any spell of up to the same level as the slot you use for the Glyph of Warding.
glyph-of-warding.name: Glyph of Warding

glyph-of-warding.components.material: Incense and powdered diamond worth 200+ GP, which the spell consumes

goodberry.description: Up to ten berries appear in your hand and are infused with magic for the duration. A creature can use its action to eat one berry. Eating a berry restores 1 hit point, and the berry provides enough nourishment to sustain a creature for one day. The berries lose their potency if they have not been consumed within 24 hours of the casting of this spell.
goodberry.name: Goodberry

grease.description: Slick grease covers the ground in a 10-foot square centered on a point within range and turns it into difficult terrain for the duration. When the grease appears, each creature standing in its area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or fall prone. A creature that enters the area or ends its turn there must also succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or fall prone.
grease.name: Grease

grease.components.material: A bit of pork rind or butter

greater-invisibility.description: You or a creature you touch has the Invisible condition until the spell ends. Whatever the target is wearing or carrying is also Invisible as long as it is on the target's person.
greater-invisibility.name: Greater Invisibility

greater-restoration.description: You imbue a creature you touch with positive energy to undo a debilitating effect. You can reduce the target's Exhaustion level by one, or end one of the following effects on the target: one effect that Charmed or Petrified the target, one curse (including the target's attunement to a cursed magic item), any reduction to one of the target's ability scores, or one effect reducing the target's Hit Point maximum.
greater-restoration.name: Greater Restoration

greater-restoration.components.material: Diamond dust worth at least 100 gp, which the spell consumes

guardian-of-faith.description: A Large spectral guardian appears and hovers for the duration in an unoccupied space of your choice that you can see within range. The guardian occupies that space and is indistinct except for a gleaming sword and shield emblazoned with the symbol of your deity. Any creature hostile to you that moves to a space within 10 feet of the guardian for the first time on a turn must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw, taking 20 Radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. The guardian vanishes when it has dealt a total of 60 damage.
guardian-of-faith.name: Guardian of Faith

guards-and-wards.description: You create a ward that protects up to 2,500 square feet of floor space. The warded area can be up to 20 feet tall. You can place the following effects: corridors filled with fog, locked doors, stinking clouds in stairs, suggestion on creature entering, and dancing lights in corridors. The whole area radiates magic. Casting this spell on the same area every day for a year makes the effect permanent.
guards-and-wards.name: Guards and Wards

guards-and-wards.components.material: Burning incense, a small measure of brimstone and oil, a knotted string, and a small amount of blood

guidance.description: You touch one willing creature. Once before the spell ends, the target can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to one ability check of its choice. It can roll the die before or after making the ability check. The spell then ends.
guidance.name: Guidance

guiding-bolt.description: A flash of light streaks toward a creature of your choice within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 4d6 radiant damage, and the next attack roll made against this target before the end of your next turn has advantage.
guiding-bolt.higherLevels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 1st.
guiding-bolt.name: Guiding Bolt

gust-of-wind.description: A line of strong wind 60 feet long and 10 feet wide blasts from you in a direction you choose for the spell's duration. Each creature that ends its turn in the line must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 15 feet away from you. Any creature in the line must spend 2 feet of movement for every 1 foot it moves toward you.
gust-of-wind.name: Gust of Wind

hallow.description: You touch a point and infuse an area around it with holy (or unholy) power. The area can have a radius up to 60 feet, and the spell fails if the radius includes an area already under the effect of a Hallow spell. Celestials, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, and Undead can't willingly enter the area. You can bind an extra effect to the area: Courage, Darkness, Daylight, Energy Protection, Energy Vulnerability, Everlasting Rest, Extradimensional Interference, Fear, Silence, or Tongues.
hallow.name: Hallow

hallow.components.material: Herbs, oils, and incense worth at least 1,000 gp, which the spell consumes

hallucinatory-terrain.description: You make natural terrain in a 150-foot Cube in range look, sound, and smell like some other sort of natural terrain. The terrain's general shape remains the same, however. Open fields or a road could be made to resemble a swamp, hill, crevasse, or some other difficult or impassable terrain. A creature that enters the area and uses its action to examine it can make an Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC to disbelieve it.
hallucinatory-terrain.name: Hallucinatory Terrain
